authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

reaches.name: Salmon Creek main stem below Highway 1
reaches.toDesc: mouth at the Pacific Ocean (Salmon Creek Beach, north of Bodega Head)

reaches.name: Salmon Creek main stem above Highway 1 and all Salmon Creek tributaries
reaches.toDesc: upstream headwaters near Occidental, and all tributaries

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(82) — Salmon Creek and tributaries (Sonoma Co.)
sources.quotedText: (82) Salmon Creek and tributaries (Sonoma Co.). Also see Section 8.00(b) Low-Flow Restrictions. (A) Salmon Creek main stem below Highway 1. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession. (B) Salmon Creek main stem above Highway 1 and all Salmon Creek tributaries. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 14 CCR §8.00(b) — Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County Coastal Streams: Special Low Flow Conditions Closures
sources.quotedText: (b) Mendocino, Sonoma, and Marin County coastal streams: Stream Closures: Special Low Flow Conditions.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

groups.humanSummary: Salmon Creek main stem below Highway 1 (Sonoma Co.): open fourth Saturday in May through March 31, artificial lures with barbless hooks only through October 31 then barbless hooks only through March 31, 2 hatchery trout/steelhead daily (4 in possession); also subject to §8.00(b) low-flow stream closures.
groups.verbatimText: (A) Salmon Creek main stem below Highway 1. Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31. Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

groups.humanSummary: Salmon Creek main stem above Highway 1 and all Salmon Creek tributaries (Sonoma Co.): closed to all fishing all year.
groups.verbatimText: (B) Salmon Creek main stem above Highway 1 and all Salmon Creek tributaries. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.description: This water is also subject to 14 CCR §8.00(b), the Mendocino/Sonoma/Marin County coastal streams special low-flow closure provision, under which fishing may be closed when streamflow at a designated gauge falls below a prescribed threshold.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to Section 8.00(b) low-flow stream closures.
Open fourth Saturday in May through March 31 on the main stem below Highway 1.
regulations.verbatimText: Fourth Sat. in May through Mar. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.

regulations.humanSummary: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Saturday in May through October 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used from the fourth Sat. in May through Oct. 31.

parameters.note: Applies from November 1 through March 31; bait is allowed on barbless hooks.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used from November 1 through March 31.
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used from Nov. 1 through Mar. 31.

parameters.counting_rule: Combined limit of hatchery trout and/or hatchery steelhead (fish showing a healed adipose fin clip); 2 fish daily, 4 fish in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily, 4 in possession, on the main stem below Highway 1.
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept below Highway 1; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing all year on the main stem above Highway 1 and on all Salmon Creek tributaries.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year above Highway 1 and on all tributaries.
regulations.verbatimText: (B) Salmon Creek main stem above Highway 1 and all Salmon Creek tributaries. Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
